task_completion.description: BlueMail application is open and the inbox is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlueMail application window is open on the screen, and the 'Inbox' view is currently displayed with a list of emails.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the creation of a new folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Folder', 'Create Folder', or similar dialog box/input field is visible within the BlueMail interface.

task_completion.description: A folder named 'Important' has been successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The folder name 'Important' is clearly visible as a distinct, newly created folder in the BlueMail folder pane or sidebar.

task_completion.description: At least two emails are selected in the inbox to be moved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two or more emails in the inbox list are highlighted, have checkmarks next to them, or show a clear selection state, often accompanied by a 'Move' menu or dialog being opened.

task_completion.description: At least two emails are successfully moved into the 'Important' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Important' folder is selected or open, and the main viewing pane displays at least two emails residing inside this specific folder.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the BlueMail graphical interface rather than using terminal commands to move files.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions (creating folders, selecting emails, moving) are performed via mouse clicks and keyboard inputs within the BlueMail GUI. No terminal windows are used to execute file manipulation commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual BlueMail application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the standard BlueMail design (window title, icons, layout) and is not a generic text editor, file explorer, or mocked webpage.

integrity.description: The agent moved existing emails rather than creating fake ones or renaming files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails shown in the 'Important' folder have realistic subjects and senders (originating from the SpamAssassin corpus) that match the emails previously seen in the inbox.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target folder must be named exactly 'Important'. The agent must move a minimum of 2 emails into this folder. The environment is populated with real-world emails from the SpamAssassin public corpus.
